summary: Linked `src/melee/it/items/itwhispyapple.c` by switching it from `NonMatching` to `Matching`, adding the Whispy Apple `ItemStateTable` data, adjusting a helper from `static inline` to `static`, and replacing a file-scope zero `Vec3` with a local zero initializer. The decomp.dev bot reported +3496 linked-code bytes, `itwhispyapple` `.data` moving from 11.11% to 100%, `itwhispyapple` `.sdata2` from 75.00% to 100%, and `itwhispyapple` `.rodata` improving from 0.00% to 85.71%. The large symbols.txt churn mostly reflects local-scope label corrections and data splitting needed for the newly matching link.
what_changed: `itwhispyapple.c` gained `ItemStateTable it_803F91D0[]` with eight entries using existing `itWhispyapple_UnkMotion{0,1,3,5,6,7}_{Anim,Phys,Coll}` callbacks and one `NULL` collision callback for the motion-3 entry.
`static Vec3 it_803B8750;` was removed and the use in `it_802EEA70` became a local `Vec3 vec = { 0, 0, 0 };`.
`itWhispyapple_UnkMotion0_Anim_inline` changed from `static inline bool` to `static bool`, likely to match the expected emitted code/layout for the linked object.

`config/GALE01/symbols.txt` was updated heavily, including many global placeholder symbols becoming local `@NNN` labels, added `...data.0` / `...bss.0` labels, and a split of `ftAnim_804590D8` from one 0x168-byte BSS object into 0x78-byte chunks.
The bot reported 5 new matches, including `main/melee/it/items/itwhispyapple` `.data` and `.sdata2`, plus associated improvements in linked code/data percentages.
smart_moves: Added the item state table directly in C rather than leaving the data unmatched; this moved `itwhispyapple` `.data` to 100% according to the bot report.

Used the existing project `ItemStateTable` pattern with callback triplets for animation, physics, and collision, preserving the observed repeated use of motion-5 handlers and the one `NULL` collision slot.
Replaced a named static zero vector with a local aggregate zero initializer, avoiding an unnecessary file-scope data symbol and matching the desired data layout better.
Changed the helper from `static inline` to `static`, a small source-level codegen lever often useful when inline decisions affect matching.
Updated the symbol map to demote many compiler-local constants/strings/floats from global-looking names to local `@NNN` labels, reducing false global symbol surface after linking.
decomp_lessons: For item files, matching may require reconstructing the `ItemStateTable` data, not just function bodies; missing state tables can keep `.data` unmatched even when callbacks already exist.
A zero `Vec3` can be better represented as a local `{ 0, 0, 0 }` initializer than as a file-scope static object when the original object did not require a persistent symbol.
`static inline` is not always harmless in matching work; if emitted code or symbol/layout expectations differ, try a plain `static` helper.

When linking a previously nonmatching object, expect broad `symbols.txt` churn because local constants, strings, jump tables, and BSS labels may need to be reclassified from placeholder globals to local compiler-style labels.
Bot match reports are useful evidence for whether data fixes were effective: here the important signal was `itwhispyapple` `.data` and `.sdata2` reaching 100%, while `.rodata` only reached 85.71%.
naming_conventions: Whispy Apple functions use the `itWhispyapple_UnkMotionN_Anim`, `itWhispyapple_UnkMotionN_Phys`, and `itWhispyapple_UnkMotionN_Coll` callback naming pattern.
Unidentified item data retained address-style names such as `it_803F91D0` and `it_803F9250` rather than speculative semantic names.
Compiler-local constants and strings in `symbols.txt` are represented as `@NNN` local labels, while synthetic section labels use forms like `...data.0` and `...bss.0`.
Build configuration uses `Object(Matching, "melee/it/items/itwhispyapple.c")` / `Object(NonMatching, ...)` to declare per-object matching status.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Promote the object to `Matching` only after source/data changes align enough to link.
Reconstruct data tables with exact entry order, flags, function-pointer callbacks, repeated callbacks, and `NULL` slots.
Use small C source transformations such as `static inline` to `static` to influence emitted function structure.
Use local aggregate initialization for zero vectors to avoid introducing or depending on a separate static data symbol.
Adjust `symbols.txt` scopes and labels after linking so local constants, strings, BSS regions, and section labels match the linker output.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
The decomp.dev bot reported `Linked code: 29.60% (+0.09%, +3496 bytes)`, `Matched data: 20.40% (+0.05%, +576 bytes)`, and `Linked data: 28.59% (+0.02%, +208 bytes)`.
The bot listed `main/melee/it/items/itwhispyapple` `.data` improving from 11.11% to 100%, `.sdata2` from 75.00% to 100%, and `.rodata` from 0.00% to 85.71%.
follow_up_queries: Find other item PRs that link by adding missing `ItemStateTable` arrays.
Search for remaining file-scope zero `Vec3` objects that might be better matched as local aggregate initializers.
Compare other item helpers where `static inline` versus `static` affects matching.
Inspect remaining unmatched `itwhispyapple` `.rodata` after this PR because the bot reported only 85.71% matched.
Look for symbols.txt updates that demote placeholder globals to local `@NNN` labels after object linking.
